reset.confirmBody: This clears your customizations for this provider so it follows the built-in defaults again. Your API key and saved sessions are kept.

reentryBanner.message: {{count}} enabled provider(s) need their API key re-entered. Open each one and add its key again.

migrationPack.barHint: Move your provider keys and subscription tokens to another machine with an encrypted pack.
migrationPack.passphraseHint: Use at least 8 characters; 12 or more is recommended.

strength.weak: Too short — use at least 8 characters.
strength.fair: OK — 12 or more characters is stronger.

export.title: Export encrypted pack
export.subtitle: Encrypt your provider keys and subscription tokens into a portable file.
export.warning: This file contains decryptable secrets. Keep it safe — anyone with the file and the passphrase can read your keys. A lost passphrase cannot be recovered.

import.title: Import encrypted pack
import.subtitle: Restore provider keys and subscription tokens from an exported pack.
import.hint: Enter the passphrase used when the pack was exported, then choose the file.
import.summary: Imported {{providerKeys}} provider key(s), {{poolKeys}} pool key(s), and {{tokenSets}} token set(s).
import.error: Wrong passphrase or corrupted pack. Nothing was changed.

providerSettings.selectPrompt: Select a provider to view details

form.name: Name *
form.chatApiFormatDescription: Select the API format that matches your provider's endpoint
form.apiKeySetPlaceholder: Key is set — leave blank to keep it
form.apiKeyReplaceHelper: A key is stored securely. Type a new value to replace it, or leave blank to keep it.
form.transformerConfigDescription: Configure transformers to adapt API request/response formats for this provider
form.transformerConfigNote: The daemon applies these transformers in the request chain, after the provider's format transformer (which handles the wire-format conversion). Unknown transformer names are skipped.
form.officialHelper: Enable if this provider connects directly to the official Anthropic API. Disable for reverse proxies or third-party services.
form.maxConcurrencyHelper: Maximum number of concurrent API requests sent to this provider. Lower values help avoid rate limiting (429 errors). Set `-1` for no limit.

codingPlan.label: Coding Plan
codingPlan.description: Use a separate API endpoint for subscription-based coding plans
codingPlan.apiKeyPlaceholder: Leave empty to use provider's API key
codingPlan.apiKeyHelper: Only required if the coding plan uses a different API key
codingPlan.tokenPlanHint: Some providers (e.g. Xiaomi MiMo) issue Token Plan keys with a `tp-` prefix — informational only, not enforced.

form.selectTypeFirstDesc: Choose what type of provider you want to add
form.codeProviderNote: Code providers only need API Key and URL. Models are managed by the Agent Router.

details.noSelection: Select a provider to view details
details.modelsEndpointHelper: Override the discovery endpoint if the provider exposes a custom /models path.
details.codeProviderNote: Code providers (like Claude Code) do not require model configuration. Models are automatically managed by the Agent Router.

apiKeyPool.title: API Key Pool
apiKeyPool.description: Configure multiple API keys for load balancing. Each session sticks to one key to preserve prompt cache.

manualDialog.title: Add Model
manualDialog.subtitle: Add a custom model to this provider
manualDialog.modelIdHelper: The exact model identifier used when calling the API
manualDialog.modelNameHelper: Optional name shown in the UI. If empty, Model ID will be used
manualDialog.groupPlaceholder: Select or enter a group name
manualDialog.helper: Provide the exact model identifier exposed by the provider API.

editDialog.title: Edit Model
editDialog.subtitle: Modify model settings
editDialog.modelIdHelper: The model identifier used when calling the API
editDialog.groupPlaceholder: Select or enter a group name
editDialog.visionHint: Whether this model supports image/vision input
editDialog.reasoningHint: Whether this model supports thinking/reasoning mode

testDialog.title: Model Connectivity Test
testDialog.unsupportedFormat: Testing isn't supported for this provider format yet.

router.title: Router Configuration
router.description: Configure which provider/model to use for different scenarios.

helper.longContextThreshold: Token count before switching to the long-context model.

agentRouter.title: Agent Router
agentRouter.description: Configure bidirectional routing between Chat and Code modes.

chatToCode.title: Chat to Code Router
chatToCode.description: Use chat providers in agent/coding mode
chatToCode.emptyState: No Chat→Code routers configured. Add one to use chat providers in coding mode.

form.name: Router Name
form.modeAdvancedDesc: Configure model mapping and context handling

mappings.title: Model Mappings
mappings.description: Map Chat provider models to Code provider models
mappings.empty: No model mappings configured. Add mappings to route chat models to code providers.

advanced.title: Advanced Configuration
advanced.description: Configure models for different task types
advanced.helpText: Default model is required. Other models are optional and will fall back to default if not configured.

default.label: Default
default.description: Primary model for general tasks (similar to Claude Sonnet)

background.label: Background
background.description: Lightweight model for background tasks (similar to Claude Haiku)

think.label: Think
think.description: Advanced model for complex reasoning (similar to Claude Opus)

image.label: Image
image.description: Model for image-related tasks with vision capability

codeToChat.title: Code to Chat Router
codeToChat.description: Expose Coding Plan models in chat mode
codeToChat.emptyState: No Code→Chat routers configured. Add one to expose Coding Plan models in chat mode.

form.name: Router Name
form.codeProviderHelper: Select the Code provider to use for transformation
form.modelsHelper: Select which Coding Plan models to expose

mappings.title: Model Mappings
mappings.description: Map Code provider models to Chat provider models for exposure
mappings.empty: No model mappings configured. Add mappings to expose Code models in chat mode.

transformers.title: Transformers
transformers.noProvider: Select a provider to configure transformers

alerts.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this provider?

errors.urlInvalid: Please enter a valid URL (e.g., https://api.example.com)
errors.deleteFailed: Error deleting provider. Please try again.
errors.toggleFailed: Error toggling provider. Please try again.

openRouter.title: OpenRouter Provider Routing
openRouter.description: Configure how requests are routed to different providers on OpenRouter.
openRouter.sortTooltip: Choose how to prioritize providers. Auto uses intelligent load balancing.
openRouter.orderTooltip: Specify providers to try in order (e.g., anthropic, openai, together). Press Enter to add.
openRouter.orderPlaceholder: Type provider slug and press Enter...
openRouter.allowFallbacksTooltip: When enabled, OpenRouter will try backup providers if the primary is unavailable.
openRouter.requireParametersTooltip: Only route to providers that support all parameters in your request.
openRouter.dataCollectionTooltip: Control whether to use providers that may store or train on your data.
openRouter.zdrTooltip: Only route to endpoints with Zero Data Retention policy.
openRouter.onlyTooltip: Only use these specific providers. Leave empty to allow all providers.
openRouter.onlyPlaceholder: Type provider slug and press Enter...
openRouter.ignoreTooltip: Skip these providers when routing requests.
openRouter.ignorePlaceholder: Type provider slug and press Enter...
openRouter.quantizationsTooltip: Only use providers with specific quantization levels. Lower precision may reduce quality but improve speed.
openRouter.maxPriceTooltip: Set maximum price limits per million tokens. Leave empty for no limit.

apiMode.label: API Mode
apiMode.apiKeyPrefixHint: Keys for this plan typically begin with `{{prefix}}`. Hint only — not enforced.
apiMode.dashscopeCodingPlanSeparateKey: DashScope Coding Plan uses a separate API key from the standard endpoint.
apiMode.baiduCodingPlanSeparateKey: Qianfan Coding Plan uses a separate API key from the standard endpoint.
apiMode.tencentTokenPlanAnthropicOnly: Tencent Token Plan is Anthropic-protocol only.
apiMode.xiaomiTokenPlanRegions: Default baseUrl is `token-plan-cn.*`. You can manually edit it to `token-plan-sgp.*` or `token-plan-ams.*`.
apiMode.confirmOverwriteBody: You have customized the API URL or API Key. Switching modes will overwrite them with the new mode's defaults — or keep your customizations and only update the mode label.

appLocal.notSupportedByDaemon: Not yet supported by the daemon
appLocal.discoveryUnsupportedFormat: Model discovery is not supported for this provider format yet. Add models manually.

apiService.title: API Service
apiService.description: Configure the outbound API server, per-endpoint model routing, and named access keys.
apiService.loadError: Could not load the server configuration. Check that the daemon is reachable.

enable.label: Enable outbound server
enable.description: Start the local API server that routes external callers through your providers.

networkBinding.label: Allow LAN access
networkBinding.description: Bind the server to your network interface instead of loopback only.
networkBinding.warning: LAN access exposes the API server to other devices on your network. Combined with subscription routing, requests can consume your subscription quota — keep this off unless you trust the network.

status.runningHint: The outbound API server is accepting requests.
status.stoppedHint: Enable the server to start accepting requests.

endpoints.title: Endpoint routing
endpoints.description: Pick the model each wire format routes to. The default model folds in here — the server is a router, not a chat app.

endpoint.subscriptionUnsupported: Subscription routing is not available for this endpoint.
endpoint.cannotStart: Service can't start: missing model mappings
endpoint.modelListEmpty: No models configured — this endpoint is unused until you add one.

keys.title: Access keys
keys.description: Named keys external callers send as a Bearer token. The full key is shown only once at creation.
keys.revokeConfirmDesc: Revoking "{{name}}" immediately stops it from authenticating. This cannot be undone.

created.title: Key created
created.warning: Copy this key now — it will not be shown again. Store it securely.

policy.title: Policy
policy.modelRestrictionEmptyAllowlistWarning: An enabled allowlist with no models denies every request.

queue.title: Request queue
queue.description: Smooth bursty traffic: serialize a user’s messages per account and cap concurrent requests per key.

serial.title: Serial message queue
serial.description: Process one real user message at a time per subscription account (FIFO). Off by default.

enable.label: Enable serial queue
enable.description: Queue overlapping user messages instead of sending them in parallel.

delayMs.label: Delay between messages (ms)
delayMs.helper: Pause after each message before the next dequeues. Range 0–10000.

waitTimeoutMs.label: Queue wait timeout (ms)
waitTimeoutMs.helper: Give up a queued message after this long. Range 1000–300000.

concurrency.title: Concurrency queue
concurrency.description: When a key hits its concurrency limit, extra requests wait in a bounded queue instead of failing outright.

maxQueueSizeFactor.label: Queue size factor
maxQueueSizeFactor.helper: Max queued × the key limit. Range 1–10.

minQueueSize.label: Min queue size
minQueueSize.helper: Floor for the per-key queue depth. Range 1–100.

waitTimeoutMs.label: Queue wait timeout (ms)
waitTimeoutMs.helper: Give up a waiting request after this long. Range 1000–300000.

proxy.title: Upstream proxy
proxy.description: Route outbound requests to upstream providers through an HTTP/HTTPS or SOCKS5 proxy. Precedence: account > provider > global > environment.

global.label: Global proxy
global.description: Applies to every upstream unless a provider or account override is set.

accounts.title: Accounts & Subscriptions
accounts.description: Manage the subscription credentials the server routes through. Tokens are written here but never displayed back.

oauth.title: Sign in to {{name}}
oauth.description: Open the sign-in page in your browser, authorize, then paste the authorization code below. The code crosses to the daemon only — the minted token is never shown.

codexOauth.title: Sign in to {{name}}
codexOauth.description: Open the sign-in page in your browser and authorize. The daemon captures the result automatically — keep this open until it completes. The token is never shown.
codexOauth.waiting: Waiting for you to complete sign-in in your browser…

accounts.clearConfirmDesc: This removes every stored credential for this provider. This cannot be undone.

list.empty: No accounts stored for this provider.
list.removeConfirmDesc: Removing "{{name}}" deletes its stored credential. This cannot be undone.
list.title: Accounts

write.title: Write {{name}} credential
write.description: Paste already-obtained token material. Fields are limited to what this provider accepts; the token is stored, never shown back.

claude.title: Claude
claude.description: Anthropic subscription credentials routed by the daemon.

codex.title: Codex
codex.description: OpenAI Codex subscription credentials routed by the daemon.

gemini.title: Gemini
gemini.description: Google Gemini subscription credentials routed by the daemon.

opencodego.title: OpenCodeGo
opencodego.description: Static API-key credentials routed by the daemon.

authMethodDesc.oauth: Sign in through your browser; the daemon captures and stores the token.
authMethodDesc.manual: Paste an already-obtained token. It is stored, never displayed back.

detail.label: Account label
detail.proxyHint: Overrides the global/provider proxy for this account only.
detail.supportedModelsHint: One model per line. `logical = actual` remaps that model to the account's upstream model; a bare list limits which models this account serves (multi-account pools only).

step1.title: Open the authorization page
step1.desc: Open the sign-in page in your browser.

step2.title: Authorize access
step2.desc: Sign in and approve access for the provider.

step3.title: Copy the authorization code
step3.desc: The page shows an authorization code after you approve.

step4.title: Paste the code
step4.desc: Paste the authorization code below to finish. The code crosses to the daemon only — the minted token is never shown.

manual.title: Paste credential
manual.description: Paste an already-obtained token. Fields are limited to what this provider accepts; the token is stored, never shown back.
manual.hint: The token is sent to the daemon and stored encrypted. It is never displayed back.

info.title: About subscription accounts
info.description: Tokens are written to the daemon and used to authenticate upstream requests. They are stored encrypted and never displayed back. Removing an account deletes its stored credential.

syncWarning.external-divergent: The external CLI login file holds a newer credential than this account. The stored token may stop refreshing — refresh now or sign in again.
syncWarning.external-not-rotated: Refresh failed, and the external CLI holds the same expired credential. Please sign in again.
syncWarning.duplicate-token: Another account shares this credential. Refreshing either account will sign the other out.

importExternal.detected: An existing {{name}} CLI login was found on this machine.
importExternal.hint: Importing takes over keeping it fresh: after each token refresh the CLI login file is updated too, so the terminal CLI stays signed in.

codeCli.title: Code CLI
codeCli.description: Point a coding CLI at the daemon's outbound proxy. This page is read-only — there is nothing to configure here.

baseUrl.title: Daemon base URL
baseUrl.description: The proxy address your CLI connects to.
baseUrl.label: Base URL

token.title: Authentication
token.hint: Send 'Authorization: Bearer <your-admin-token>' for admin routes. The admin token is configured in the daemon (its config file or environment) — it is never shown here.

examples.title: Example launch environment
examples.keyNote: Use an outbound access key created on the API Service page — not the admin token.

codeCli.launchDescription: Launch a coding CLI in a terminal, pointed at this daemon. The daemon injects the redirect env and starts the CLI for you.

cli.notFoundHint: This CLI isn't on the daemon host's PATH. Install it, then refresh.
cli.installingHint: Installing on the daemon host — this can take a minute.
cli.launchDescription: Opens a new terminal on the daemon host running this CLI, routed through the daemon proxy. Pick an optional working directory.

manual.title: Manual setup
manual.description: Prefer to run the CLI yourself? Point it at the daemon with these env vars.

daemonStatus.failedHint: Ensure Node.js is installed and the daemon is built (dist present), or set OMNICROSS_DAEMON_ENTRY to the daemon's cli.js.

settings.title: Settings
settings.desktopOnly: Startup and tray options apply only in the desktop app.

startup.title: Startup
startup.autoLaunchHint: Start Omnicross automatically when you sign in.
startup.startMinimizedHint: Launch in the background without opening the window.

tray.title: Tray
tray.minimizeOnCloseHint: Closing the window hides it to the tray instead of quitting.

usageStats.title: Usage Statistics
usageStats.description: Token and cost statistics for requests served by the daemon.
usageStats.unpricedHint: No pricing entry exists for this model — add one on the Pricing page.
usageStats.unattributedHint: Requests not linked to a specific API key. This is expected until per-key attribution is recorded.
usageStats.dashboardDescription: Overview, model distribution, and usage trends for requests served by the daemon.

pricing.title: Model Pricing
pricing.description: Per-model token prices used to compute request costs, in USD per 1M tokens.
pricing.emptyHint: Fetch the latest prices to populate it.
pricing.conflictHint: These entries were edited by you and also changed at the source. Choose per row whether to keep your value (Skip) or take the incoming one (Overwrite).
pricing.resolvedWithStale: {{overwritten}} overwritten, {{skipped}} skipped. {{stale}} changed meanwhile and were left untouched.
pricing.refreshFailed: The change was applied, but reloading the list failed: {{message}}

proxy.saveHint: Upstream requests route through this proxy.
proxy.disabledHint: No proxy — upstream requests go direct.
